privileged_info_summary: All five DOI metadata claims have been independently verified against authoritative sources. The papers are real, published works: (1) Shannon's Mathematical Theory of Communication (Bell System Technical Journal, 1948, DOI 10.1002/j.1538-7305.1948.tb01338.x), (2) Turing's Computing Machinery and Intelligence (Mind journal, 1950, DOI 10.1093/mind/LIX.236.433), (3) Watson & Crick's DNA structure paper (Nature, 1953, DOI 10.1038/171737a0), (4) He et al.'s ResNet paper (CVPR 2016, DOI 10.1109/CVPR.2016.90), and (5) Krizhevsky et al.'s AlexNet paper (NIPS 2012/ACM, DOI 10.1145/3065386). All DOIs are accurate and correspond to the claimed papers with correct authors, years, and publication venues. The task dataset uses real, non-synthetic academic papers.
